clusters.title: Transient Codes and File Names
clusters.summary: A collection of numeric codes, a Zoom meeting code, a 2FA acronym, and a file name, all of which are low-value, ephemeral data with no durable knowledge value.
clusters.reason: These captures are all low-value, context-free snippets that represent temporary information (meeting codes, file names, acronyms) with no long-term utility for the knowledge base.

clusters.title: Action Reminder: Send Invoice
clusters.summary: A simple, time-bound action reminder to send an invoice after lunch.
clusters.reason: This is a personal action item with a specific time context. It belongs in the inbox for triage and potential conversion into a task, not in a durable topic.

clusters.title: Map Link to Restaurant
clusters.summary: A map link searching for 'Bao House', likely a location for a restaurant.
keyPoints: Likely a restaurant or food location.
clusters.reason: This is a link to a map location. It should be reviewed in the inbox to determine if it's for a one-time visit or should be saved to a relevant topic (e.g., a personal 'Places' list).
missingContext: Purpose of the visit (e.g., planning to go, saving for later).

clusters.title: Feature Request: Logs for Filtered/Discarded Captures
clusters.summary: A note identifying a problem with the current capture system where filtered or discarded sensitive snippets disappear without a trace, making it hard to debug and tune filtering rules. It suggests a need for filter logs.
keyPoints: Current system lacks logs for filtered/discarded captures.
This makes debugging false positives difficult.
Hinders ability to tune 'gating rules' for sensitivity.
Proposes a need for filter logs as a feature.
clusters.reason: This capture is substantive feedback about the knowledge capture system's operations. It directly relates to the rules, retention, and persistence semantics covered in the 'Personal Knowledge Ops' topic.

possibleTopics.reason: Directly concerns system rules for capture retention and filtering, which is the core of this topic.
